title: rhus aromatica var. suaveolens
intro: Rhus aromatica var. suaveolens, also known as Fragrant Sumac, is a shrub native to North America that is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used to create herbal preparations that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Fragrant Sumac have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Fragrant Sumac can be made into teas, salves, and infusions, and are often used to promote health and wellness due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
